content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers along with QQQ queries.

For each query:


XXX : Given an integer XXX, print and **remove** the **minimum** element in the array kkk, such that gcd(X,k)>1\texttt{gcd} (X, k) \gt 1gcd(X,k)>1, where gcd\texttt{gcd}gcd denotes the greatest common divisor.

If no value present in the array satisfies the condition, print and remove the **minimum** element of the array.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case consists of a single integer NNN — the number of elements in the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of the array AAA.
The third line contains one integer QQQ, denoting the number of queries.
The next line contains QQQ space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer is XiX_iXi​, denoting the ithi^{th}ith query.




Output Format
For each test case, print QQQ space-separated integers denoting the answer to each query.
constraints: 1 ≤ Q ≤ N ≤ 2·10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5, sum of Q over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains N, the size of the array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN. Third line contains Q, the number of queries. Fourth line contains Q space‑separated integers X1 … XQ.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output Q space‑separated integers on a single line – the value printed for each query in the order they are processed.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The given array is [2,9,3,15,10][2, 9, 3, 15, 10][2,9,3,15,10] and there are 444 queries:

Query 111: Given X=2X = 2X=2. The elements 222 and 101010 satisfy the condition. Since 222 is smaller, we print and remove 222 from array. The array becomes A=[9,3,15,10]A = [9, 3, 15, 10]A=[9,3,15,10].
Query 222: Given X=2X = 2X=2. The element 101010 satisfies the condition. We print and remove 101010 from array. The array becomes A=[9,3,15]A = [9, 3, 15]A=[9,3,15].
Query 333: Given X=3X = 3X=3. The elements 333 and 999 satisfy the condition. Since 333 is smaller, we print and remove 333 from array. The array becomes A=[9,15]A = [9, 15]A=[9,15].
Query 444: Given X=7X = 7X=7. No element satisfies the condition, so we have to select 999 since it is the smallest element present. We print and remove 999 from array. The array becomes A=[15]A = [15]A=[15].

solution.approach:
Pre‑compute the smallest prime factor (SPF) for every number up to 10^6 using a linear sieve. For each test case keep:
1. cnt[v] – how many copies of value v are still present.
2. a global min‑heap (priority_queue with smallest on top) containing every element.
3. For every prime p a separate min‑heap that stores all elements divisible by p.
When reading the initial array, factorise each element into its distinct prime factors using the SPF table and push the element into the global heap and into each heap of its prime factors.
For a query X, factorise X into distinct primes P. For each p in P look at the top of heap[p] and discard elements whose cnt is zero (lazy deletion). The smallest among those tops is the candidate that shares a divisor >1 with X. If no such candidate exists, clean the global heap in the same lazy way and take its top. Output the chosen value, decrement its cnt, and the element will be ignored in future heap clean‑ups.
All operations on a heap are O(log size). Each element is inserted once per its distinct prime factor and removed at most once from each heap, so the total work is O((N+Q)·log N) plus the O(MAX log log MAX) sieve. This easily fits the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O((N + Q) · log N + MAX_A · log log MAX_A) per test file, where MAX_A = 10^6.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N · ω(A) + MAX_A) where ω(A) is the maximum number of distinct prime factors of any Ai (≤7 for 10^6).
